reason: The justifications are well-supported by fan/critic consensus. The St. Mungo's scene omission, reduced DoM battle agency, compressed motivations tied to his parents' fate, and loss of internal emotional texture are all widely documented complaints. The broken nose detail in the DoM battle is book-accurate. The one mild weakness: the score of 10/25 for motivations feels slightly harsh given the films do convey his core motivation (courage, loyalty, standing up to Voldemort) even if the parents-as-driver is stripped out - some fans would rate this higher. The arc score of 13/25 is also on the low end given the films do show his transformation from bumbling student to hero, even if the book's richer internal journey is lost. Overall the score and reasoning are consistent with the broad fan/critical view that Neville is one of the more significantly shortchanged characters in the adaptations, making 74/100 a reasonable and defensible number.
